signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Blowout Jobs Data
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.7017 following a massive upside surprise in Australian employment, which added 76.3k jobs against a 15.0k forecast. RBA holds at 4.35%. The G10 Currency Scorecard confirms AUD as the week's dominant leader while CHF lags, creating a clear bilateral divergence. Technically, AUD/USD is holding above its SMA20 (0.6945) but remains capped by the SMA50 at 0.7041. The options market shows an elevated vol premium with AUD/USD IV at 12.2% vs HV30 at 6.4%, suggesting traders are paying up for protection amid this shift in rate expectations. Risk: A significant miss in upcoming Q2 CPI data that forced the RBA to abandon its hike bias.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry vs Options Skew + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 163.05 remains supported by a dominant +275bp Fed-BoJ carry differential, even as the pair trades above its SMA20/50/200 cluster. Despite the carry advantage, the options market is pricing in heavy downside protection with a 25d Risk Reversal of -1.45 (put skew) and an elevated Vol Risk Premium of +3.5% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.7%). This skew reflects acute institutional fear of BoJ intervention or hawkish signaling at the upcoming 31 July meeting, despite the bearish spec positioning (COT LF net short -105,238). Retail sentiment is also extreme at 84% short, which typically suggests further upside exhaustion if price cannot clear recent highs. Risk: Formal BoJ intervention or a surprise rate hike that triggers a disorderly carry unwind.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Vol Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: EUR/USD trades at 1.1421, positioned below its SMA20 (1.1417) and SMA50 (1.1519) as the ECB prepares for its meeting today. While the ECB is technically in a hiking cycle at 2.40%, a strong negative Economic Surprise Index (CESI net -20) contrasts with the USD's strong positive score (net +24), weighing on the pair's fundamental support. The options market displays a notable Vol Risk Premium of +2.4% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.7%), indicating elevated hedging demand ahead of the ECB decision. COT data shows specs are already net short EUR (-74,637), suggesting the market is positioned for a dovish hold or a pivot in forward guidance. Risk: ECB delivers a hawkish surprise that forces an unwind of the established spec short.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Carry Advantage
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4074 is exhibiting a significant correlation break against WTI Oil, with a 252d z-score of -1.81σ (current corr -0.923 vs norm +0.027). This suggests CAD is failing to benefit from rising energy prices, with WTI up +1.52% to $88.2. Instead, the pair is being driven by the +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and a heavily crowded speculative short in CAD (COT LF net -93,515). Technically, the pair is holding below its SMA20 (1.4149) but remains well above the SMA50 (1.3998). This divergence implies that unless BoC bias shifts hawkishly, CAD will remain decoupled from its traditional oil anchor. Risk: BoC signals a surprise return to tightening in response to sticky inflation, revitalizing the CAD bid.
